AC5: '! grep -q \'route_inbound_probe stays here\' … && ! grep -q \'reconcile.py keeps\' …' — confirms removal only

findings.finding: AC3, AC4, and AC5 only verify that OLD content is removed; they do not verify that the REPLACEMENT content specified in What items 3, 4, and 5 was actually written. An executor could delete the targeted sections/comments entirely (or write incorrect replacement text) and all three ACs would still pass.
scenarios: Executor deletes the entire '## Module-size policy' section in architecture.md rather than updating it — AC3 greens.
Executor deletes the stale comments in reconcile.py and run_differs.py without adding replacement comments — AC5 greens.
Executor removes allowlist mention from AGENTS.md but omits the 'cap is absolute and admin-locked' language — AC4 greens.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove What item 5 (stale reconcile residency comment fixes for 3c7f) from this leaf's scope — it is not covered by the parent epic (716f) — and either deliver it directly on ticket 3c7f or update the parent epic to include this scope before re-reviewing the leaf.
evidence: Parent Decomposition: 'Phase 2 — one child story: delete the allowlist+ceilings mechanism, replace the contract test with an absolute 'nothing over 800' test, prune the architecture.md offender table, and add the limit-lock gate.' — no mention of reconcile comment fixes.

Parent file_impact lists: .github/module-size-allowlist.txt, .github/module-size-ceilings.txt, tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py, docs/architecture.md, .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml, .github/workflows/test.yml — neither reconcile.py nor run_differs.py appears.
Leaf file_impact adds: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py and src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py — not present in parent.
Leaf plan itself flags item 5 as 'Incidental (unblocks 3c7f)' — explicitly acknowledging it belongs to a different ticket's scope.
findings.finding: Leaf What item 5 — fixing stale residency comments in reconcile.py and run_differs.py to unblock ticket 3c7f — is entirely outside the parent epic's declared scope. The parent's Decomposition, ACs, and file_impact are exclusively about the module-size allowlist/ceilings mechanism; reconcile.py, run_differs.py, route_inbound_probe, and ticket 3c7f appear nowhere in the parent plan.
scenarios: An implementer following both the leaf and parent plans would conclude the reconcile comment changes are sanctioned by the parent — they are not, since the parent never covers this work.
If the parent epic is reviewed for completeness, the reconcile comment fixes will not be accounted for, leaving a gap in the epic's delivery record.
If this leaf is reverted for any reason, the 3c7f fix is also reverted, but the parent epic's acceptance criteria are unaffected — indicating the work is truly out-of-scope.
findings.suggested_fix: Move item 5 and its two ACs (the `! grep -q 'route_inbound_probe stays here'` and `! grep -q 'reconcile.py keeps'` checks) to ticket 3c7f directly, since that ticket is the stated blocker. Alternatively, if bundling is genuinely desired, update parent 716f's Decomposition and file_impact to include the reconcile comment fix before re-reviewing this leaf.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out / rollback note (e.g. 'to revert: `git revert <this commit>` restores the two files; re-check that Phase 2a gate is re-wired to read them before pushing') to satisfy T4's reversibility requirement.
evidence: Why: 'Those two files are now orphaned (empty of module entries, read by nothing). Delete them'
Notes: 'Mechanical deletion + doc/comment edits; no runtime code logic changes'
ABSENCE: no 'rollback', 'revert', 'back-out', or 'restore' language appears anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan performs irreversible destructive operations (git rm of two tracked files) but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan — only a sequencing prerequisite and justification. T4 requires a reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement of the breakage and its justification.
findings.location: Why section ('git rm .github/module-size-allowlist.txt and .github/module-size-ceilings.txt'); Notes ('Mechanical deletion + doc/comment edits')
scenarios: Phase 2a (5f9d) is later reverted or found to still read the files in an edge-case CI path; this change has already deleted them with no documented recovery procedure.
A post-merge incident requires reverting this change; the implementer has no documented steps.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection to Notes: 'To back out: `git revert <this commit>`. Before pushing the revert, confirm Phase 2a's gate is also reverted so it again reads the restored files; otherwise CI will fail on missing file references.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider splitting item 5 (the stale reconcile residency comment fix for 3c7f) into its own micro-ticket so the escape-hatch retirement and the comment fix can be reviewed/reverted independently — note this is advisory given the mechanical nature of both changes.
evidence: 'Incidental (unblocks 3c7f): update the two stale residency comments' — the plan itself flags this as a different concern tied to a distinct ticket
Item 5 touches src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py and run_differs.py — unrelated to .github/ or docs/
The ACs for item 5 ('! grep -q ...' in reconcile.py/run_differs.py) are independently verifiable from the file-deletion ACs
Closing 3c7f is explicitly listed as a separate blocker: 'Closing 3c7f is blocked on these'
findings.finding: The plan bundles the escape-hatch retirement (items 1–4, driven by the module-size gate change) with an incidental stale-comment fix in reconcile.py/run_differs.py (item 5, closing a different ticket 3c7f). These two outcomes have different reasons-to-change and are independently valuable and releasable.
scenarios: If the file-deletion PR needs to be reverted or held, the comment fix is also blocked, keeping 3c7f open unnecessarily
Review feedback on the comment fix could hold up the more urgent escape-hatch cleanup

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that Phase 2a (commit 5f9d) has landed on main and that both `.github/workflows/test.yml` and `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` no longer reference `module-size-allowlist.txt` or `module-size-ceilings.txt` before executing this plan.

The plan itself says this change must land AFTER Phase 2a (5f9d), implying Phase 2a's changes are a prerequisite — but those changes are not visible in the current repo.
findings.finding: The plan's core premise — 'Phase 2a swapped the CI gate to an absolute cap that reads NEITHER the allowlist NOR the ceilings file' — is contradicted by the current codebase. Both `.github/workflows/test.yml` (lines 208-209) and `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (lines 164-165) still actively read both files. If Phase 2a has not landed yet, deleting the files in this change will break CI exactly as the plan warns against.
findings.location: Why section: 'Phase 2a swapped the CI Module-size gate to an absolute cap + admin-locked limit that reads NEITHER the allowlist nor the ceilings file'
scenarios: If this plan is executed against the current repo (Phase 2a not yet merged), deleting the two files will make both CI workflows fail immediately on the `while read -r path ceiling` loop and the allowlist grep.
The plan's sequencing guard ('This must land AFTER Phase 2a') is unverifiable from the plan text alone — an executor cannot confirm Phase 2a has landed.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit AC: `! grep -q 'module-size-allowlist\|module-size-ceilings' .github/workflows/test.yml .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` as a pre-condition gate the executor must pass before proceeding with any deletions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the What section an explicit step to update `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py`'s `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` to remove the `'module-size ratchet': 'module-size-ceilings.txt'` entry (or update it to reflect the new gate), so AC6's `ci_workflow_parity` assertion is achievable.

findings.finding: The plan's AC6 asserts the `ci_workflow_parity` test suite will pass green after this change, but `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` line 50 contains `'module-size ratchet': 'module-size-ceilings.txt'` in `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES`. Deleting `module-size-ceilings.txt` without updating this parity test (which the plan does not specify) would cause the parity test to fail since the signature it checks for would no longer be valid.
findings.location: AC6: 'The full unit suite is green — no test/reference still expects the deleted files (verify: `pytest tests/unit -q -k 'module_size or ci_workflow_parity'`)'
scenarios: Executor deletes ceilings.txt, runs `pytest tests/unit -q -k 'ci_workflow_parity'`, test fails because the workflow signature `module-size-ceilings.txt` no longer matches the updated workflow (if Phase 2a changed the workflows) or fails because the file is gone.
If Phase 2a updated the workflow but this test was not updated, the parity test would already be failing on main.
findings.suggested_fix: In What §2 (test file updates), add: 'Update `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` in `test_ci_workflow_parity.py` to remove the `module-size ratchet` entry or replace it with the new absolute-cap gate signature from Phase 2a.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in What item 2 that the four existing tests (test_allowlist_equals_computed_over_cap_set, test_membership_and_ceilings_list_the_same_paths, test_allowlisted_files_within_pinned_ceilings, test_every_allowlist_entry_is_a_real_file) must be removed/replaced — not just that a new absence-assertion test is added.

Plan says 'add a test asserting the allowlist + ceilings files no longer exist' — no mention of deleting the existing tests that require the files to be present
findings.finding: test_module_size_contract.py contains four existing tests (test_allowlist_equals_computed_over_cap_set, test_membership_and_ceilings_list_the_same_paths, test_allowlisted_files_within_pinned_ceilings, test_every_allowlist_entry_is_a_real_file) that call read_membership(ALLOWLIST) and read_ceilings(CEILINGS) and will raise FileNotFoundError once the files are deleted. The plan says only 'add a test asserting the allowlist + ceilings files no longer exist' without specifying that these four existing tests must be removed or replaced.
scenarios: Executor adds the absence-assertion test but leaves the four existing tests intact; pytest fails with FileNotFoundError on the four old tests, breaking AC 'the full unit suite is green'
The AC only verifies pytest passes on test_module_size_contract.py with -q, which would catch this — but only if the executor runs it; the plan gives no guidance on which existing tests to remove
findings.suggested_fix: Revise What item 2 to: 'Replace all four existing tests that open the allowlist/ceilings files with a single test asserting both files are absent (the escape hatch is gone for good). Remove: test_allowlist_equals_computed_over_cap_set, test_membership_and_ceilings_list_the_same_paths, test_allowlisted_files_within_pinned_ceilings, test_every_allowlist_entry_is_a_real_file. Add: test_allowlist_and_ceilings_files_absent.'

findings.finding: test_ci_workflow_parity.py has _SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES = {"module-size ratchet": "module-size-ceilings.txt", ...}. If Phase 2a replaced the gate with one that reads .github/module-size-limit.txt instead of module-size-ceilings.txt, this signature will no longer appear in both CI workflows after Phase 2b (because neither file exists). The plan does not enumerate this test as a consumer to update, nor does AC 6 ('full unit suite is green') name this file explicitly.
scenarios: If Phase 2a changed the CI gate signature (e.g. to reference module-size-limit.txt), the parity test would fail because 'module-size-ceilings.txt' no longer appears in either workflow — this change breaks test_ci_workflow_parity without the plan ever mentioning it must be updated
AC 6 runs this test class (-k ci_workflow_parity) so the failure would be caught — but the plan gives no guidance on what the update should be

evidence: What item 2 says only: 'add a test asserting the allowlist + ceilings files no longer exist' — it does not say to remove the four existing tests that open those files.
test_module_size_contract.py:35-36: ALLOWLIST = REPO_ROOT / '.github' / 'module-size-allowlist.txt'; CEILINGS = REPO_ROOT / '.github' / 'module-size-ceilings.txt'
test_module_size_contract.py:89: allowed = read_membership(ALLOWLIST)  — will raise FileNotFoundError after deletion
test_module_size_contract.py:109: membership = read_membership(ALLOWLIST)  — will raise FileNotFoundError after deletion
test_module_size_contract.py:126-136: read_ceilings(CEILINGS) call  — will raise FileNotFoundError after deletion

findings.finding: Deleting the two files without specifying removal/replacement of the four existing tests in `test_module_size_contract.py` that read those files (test_allowlist_equals_computed_over_cap_set, test_membership_and_ceilings_list_the_same_paths, test_allowlisted_files_within_pinned_ceilings, test_every_allowlist_entry_is_a_real_file) will produce FileNotFoundError on every test that calls read_membership(ALLOWLIST) or read_ceilings(CEILINGS), breaking AC2 and AC6.
findings.location: What item 2 / tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py lines 83–145 / AC2 / AC6
scenarios: An implementer adds only the absence-assertion test as specified and deletes the files; the four pre-existing tests crash with FileNotFoundError, reddening AC2 and AC6.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend What item 2 to: 'Rewrite `test_module_size_contract.py`: remove the four existing tests that read the deleted files (and their `ALLOWLIST`/`CEILINGS` constants and `read_membership`/`read_ceilings` helpers); replace with (a) a test asserting both files are absent and (b) a test asserting no Python file under `src/rebar` exceeds 800 LOC (the new absolute-cap oracle), keeping `compute_over_cap_modules` and `test_compute_over_cap_is_deterministic`.'  Update AC2 accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a What item to update `test_ci_workflow_parity.py`'s `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` — replace `"module-size ratchet": "module-size-ceilings.txt"` with a signature string that the new absolute-cap gate actually contains (e.g. `module-size-limit.txt` per Phase 2a, or the new gate's distinguishing token), so `test_verified_gate_runs_every_shared_gate` stays green under AC6.

findings.finding: The `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` dict in `test_ci_workflow_parity.py` currently contains `"module-size ratchet": "module-size-ceilings.txt"`. Since Phase 2a already replaced the CI gate with one that no longer references `module-size-ceilings.txt`, that signature string is absent from both workflow files. After this phase's file deletions land, `test_verified_gate_runs_every_shared_gate` will fail because `module-size-ceilings.txt` no longer appears in `test.yml` or `gerrit-verify.yaml`, yet AC6 explicitly runs `pytest tests/unit -q -k 'module_size or ci_workflow_parity'`.
scenarios: Implementer completes all five What items; CI runs AC6; test_verified_gate_runs_every_shared_gate fails because 'module-size-ceilings.txt' is not in either workflow file, reddening AC6.
